verdicts.fix_sketch: Refractory anaphylaxis after 2 IM doses; IV infusion vs IV bolus is genuine paediatric discriminator. Other distractors weaker but adequate.
Fluid-refractory septic shock with hepatomegaly hint. Noradrenaline-vs-adrenaline first-line in paeds and fluid-overload recognition are AU-specific L4 discriminators.

Recognising NAI in pre-mobile infant is single-concept. Distractors B (reassure), D (no notification), E (confront mother) are absurd; A (outpatient coags only) and C (correct) are the only credible pair. Relabel to L3. Optional rewrite: replace weak distractors with credible alternatives such as 'admit overnight for observation without imaging' or 'urgent paediatric review tomorrow with FBC/coag only'.

Lead-in asks 'best reflects management priorities' so bundled option is technically OK, but distractors (withdraw care, hyperventilate, rapid rewarm to 38+ceftriaxone, early extubation) are caricatures. Rewrite distractors to credible alternatives: e.g. 'controlled rewarming to 36 then maintain normothermia 24-48h vs targeted temperature management 33 degC for 24h'; 'permissive hypercapnia at FiO2 0.4 vs FiO2 1.0 for 24h'; 'discuss ECMO at H4 vs deferred ECMO discussion'. Otherwise relabel to L3.

Single-step recall: level above treatment line = NAC. Distractors C (charcoal at 5h), D (discharge above line), E (methionine + transplant) are clearly absurd. Only A (repeat at 8h) is partially plausible. Either relabel to L3, or rewrite with credible distractors such as 'one-bag 12-h NAC vs two-bag 20-h NAC' (AU dosing nuance) or 'modified massive ingestion protocol with double-strength NAC'.
GINA step-up at age 10 from low-dose ICS uncontrolled; ICS-LABA vs adding LTRA vs SMART is genuine current-guideline discrimination.

CF exacerbation with known Pseudomonas; dual antipseudomonal (ceftazidime + tobramycin) is the established CF standard. Meropenem-monotherapy and oral cipro distractors test the 'mono vs dual' principle - genuine L4.

Lead-in says 'single most appropriate next step' but option D bundles four discrete actions (document Gillick, maintain confidentiality, encourage parental involvement, refer). Fix: either change lead-in to 'Which best describes appropriate management' OR shorten D to 'Document Gillick competence and refer to the patient's chosen termination pathway in confidence'. Distractors otherwise legally plausible-enough.

Lead-in explicitly asks for 'single most appropriate combination of statutory pathways' so bundled answer is congruent. Vic-specific Reportable Conduct Scheme knowledge with credible factual traps (A misstates RCS scope) - genuine L4.
Refeeding-rate cut-off (20 vs 50 kcal/kg/d), medical vs MH ward placement, and IV bolus risk in low-weight AN are credible L4 traps - all current AU eating disorder admission policy.

Each option bundles drug + monitoring + (sometimes) CBT decision. Option E bundles correct drug (sertraline is second-line in AU paeds MDD per RCH) with an absurd 'stop CBT' tag, breaking the genuine fluoxetine-vs-sertraline discrimination. Fix: separate monitoring into the lead-in (every option states '+ weekly SI review for 4 weeks'), then options vary only on agent + dose. Restructure E to 'Commence sertraline 25 mg daily titrating to 50 mg' without the stop-CBT distraction. D (amitriptyline) is the only fully implausible option; A/B/C/E should be drug-only discriminators.
Stage 1 (GnRH analogue) vs Stage 2 (testosterone) timing AND post-Re-Kelvin removal of Family Court requirement are both AU-specific L4 knowledge. Genuine L4.

Lead-in asks for 'disposition and observation plan' so bundling is congruent. Option B (fixed 4h regardless of severity) is a credible ASCIA-tiered observation trap; severity-based 6-12h vs 4h is genuine L4 knowledge.

Distractor A (prednisolone 6 weeks no taper), D (adrenaline twice weekly), E (ciclosporin first) are caricatures and ruled out without thought. Replace with credible alternatives: 'Switch to fexofenadine 180 mg daily at standard dose then review'; 'Add montelukast 10 mg daily as second-line add-on'; 'Refer for omalizumab now without further antihistamine titration'. Up-titration before omalizumab is the correct step but needs plausible competitors.

Recognising necrotising fasciitis (pain out of proportion + dusky post-varicella + shock) is single-step. Distractors A (continue oral aciclovir), C (topical mupirocin), D (IVIG alone), E (MRI before treatment) are absurd. Either relabel L3 or rewrite distractors to credible competing regimens: 'IV ceftriaxone monotherapy without clindamycin' (misses toxin suppression); 'IV flucloxacillin + gentamicin only' (misses GAS toxin coverage); 'IV pip-taz + vancomycin' (no clinda); 'theatre first, antibiotics intraoperatively' (delays time-critical antibiotics).

Drug + targetoid + Nikolsky + mucositis = SJS/TEN pattern recognition (single-step). Distractor B (continue Bactrim at half dose) is absurd; E (biopsy before therapy) absurd; D (Kawasaki at age 8 with drug exposure) very weak. C (SSSS) is the only credible morphological trap. Rewrite distractors to credible SJS/TEN management nuances: AGEP look-alike management; immediate IVIG vs ciclosporin vs supportive-only debate; transfer to burns vs general PICU; SCORTEN-stratified disposition.

All four distractors encode specific 2020 RHDAustralia Jones-criteria knowledge errors (fever mandatory; subclinical carditis required; monoarthritis required; anti-DNase B doesn't count). Excellent AU-specific L4.
Methylphenidate vs atomoxetine first-line is genuine guideline-discrimination; elimination-diet trap is plausible (Cochrane-evidence question). Option E (stress echo) is weak but adequate.

Three of four distractors (confidentiality regardless, involuntary admission, reassure as normal) are absurd. Single-concept recognition. Optional rewrite: replace with credible competing plans such as 'admit to general paediatric ward overnight for safety plan + CAMHS in-reach next day' or 'commence fluoxetine 10 mg with weekly review + parallel CAMHS referral and continued GP safety planning'.

Cautious refeeding rate (5-10 kcal/kg) and prophylactic Pi/thiamine are genuine knowledge but A (outpatient with HR 38) and B (olanzapine + discharge) are absurd. C (2000 kcal/d) and D (psych ward) are credible traps. Relabel L5 -> L4; consider rewriting A, B to credible alternatives like 'admit to MH unit with medical ward in-reach' or 'NG continuous feeds at 80 kcal/kg/d from day 1'.

ConSEPT/ECLiPSE-era APLS status update (levetiracetam 40 mg/kg as second-line, displacing phenytoin) is high-yield current-evidence L4. Distractors A (phenytoin), B (phenobarb) test prior-era knowledge - genuine traps.
Severe asthma escalation pathway: IV Mg vs IV aminophylline vs IV salbutamol vs intubation is the genuine paediatric ED escalation hierarchy - good L4.

Correct option C bundles three actions (hypertonic saline + reduce fluid + CT), but lead-in 'single most appropriate next step' is mildly mismatched. Acceptable because hypertonic saline IS the single immediate intervention and the others are logistics. Optional rewrite: trim C to 'IV hypertonic 3% sodium chloride 5 mL/kg over 10 minutes' as the single immediate action.

Staggered paracetamol = nomogram inapplicable, give NAC. All four distractors (plot nomogram, withhold for normal LFTs, charcoal at 18 h, methionine) are clearly wrong. Single-step recall once staggered-ingestion concept is invoked. Optional rewrite: replace distractors with credible NAC regimen alternatives - 'two-bag 20 h vs three-bag 21 h regimen', 'extended NAC beyond 21 h if ALT rising', 'massive ingestion double-dose NAC'.

Near-duplicate of paeds-emerg-005 (DKA cerebral oedema). Distractors A (increase fluids + rebolus), B (stop insulin), D (bicarbonate), E (LP) are all clearly wrong given the Cushing's-reflex pattern. Either dedupe with paeds-emerg-005, or rewrite distractors to credible alternatives: '20% mannitol 0.5-1 g/kg vs 3% saline' (genuine choice in some protocols), 'reduce fluid rate AND insulin together', 'urgent CT before any pharmacological intervention'.
GnRH stim test for central vs peripheral PP is high-yield; 17-OH progesterone (CAH) and MRI traps are credible. Good L4.
2011 AAP statement reversed routine flex-ext films in T21 - tests current-guideline knowledge against outdated practice. Genuine L4.
Williams + hypercalcaemia: stop Vit D + low-Ca formula. Pamidronate (severe-only) and frusemide (no longer recommended) traps are genuine. Good L4.
Marfan with rapidly enlarging root: surgical threshold (4 vs 5 cm) and sport restriction nuance are genuine L4 discriminations.
Lead-in is plural ('diagnosis AND best next step'), so bundling is congruent. vEDS-specific celiprolol + contraindicated arteriography (vessel fragility) are credible L4 traps.
TSC + IS = vigabatrin (vs prednisolone for non-TSC IS) is a published guideline-specific discriminator. Genuine L4.

NEC pattern recognition (preterm + pneumatosis + portal venous gas + bilious aspirates) - all four distractors absurd (no abx, continue feeds + probiotics, laparotomy regardless, peritoneal drain alone). Optional rewrite: distractor alternatives could discriminate which antibiotic regimen, when to add antifungal cover, peritoneal drain vs laparotomy for free gas, timing of surgical consult.
ESPGHAN 2020 no-biopsy pathway vs AU still-biopsy practice is genuine current-evidence discriminator. Distractor B is the plausible competitor.

SCID pattern recognition - distractors C (reassure), D (MMR live vaccine - would kill the child), E (HIV Ab at 4 months - useless due to maternal Ab), are absurd. A (fluconazole + 4-week review) is the only weakly plausible alternative. Optional rewrite: replace with credible alternatives such as 'send SCID panel + immunology referral at 4 weeks', 'add cotrimoxazole prophylaxis + outpatient immunology review', 'commence IVIG empirically + admit'.
HSV encephalitis temporal lobe features (olfactory hallucination + dysphasia) + empirical aciclovir before PCR; bacterial-vs-viral and anti-NMDAR-vs-HSV distractors are credible L4 traps.
Mantoux cut-offs for high-risk contacts and INH 9-mo vs 4R rifampicin alternative are AU-specific L4 knowledge. IGRA-under-5s trap is credible.
VZIG window (96 h ideal, up to 10 d in immunocompromised) vs prophylactic oral antivirals - genuine ATAGI/Australian Immunisation Handbook discriminator. Live vaccine trap correctly absurd-by-design.
Cefepime-mono vs pip-taz + gent vs meropenem-mono in FN shock is genuine guideline-variation L4. Vanco-when-shock-persists nuance is high-yield.
Kocher-4/4 + aspirate-then-antibiotic sequencing is the AU paeds ED L4 discriminator. MRI-before-aspiration is the credible trap.

Mastoiditis with CT temporal bones and surgical consult; B (admit + IV fluclox alone) is the genuine plausible-but-wrong competitor (misses ceftriaxone for typical mastoiditis flora and the CT imaging step).

Neonatal gonococcal conjunctivitis day 3 (with risk-factor history) - chemical (day 1), HSV ophthalmic and Chlamydia oral-azithro distractors test correct timeline + antibiotic class + route nuance. Good L4.

Lead-in 'immediate management sequence' is plural so bundling is congruent. Kingella kingae cefazolin add-on is high-yield AU paeds ID, and 10 vs 20 mL/kg bolus + abx-vs-MRI timing nuances are L5-appropriate.

Near-duplicate of paeds-gisurg-008 (NEC). All four distractors absurd. Consider deduping or rewriting distractors with NEC-specific competing decisions (Bell stage threshold for surgical consult, peritoneal drain vs laparotomy for free gas, when to add fluconazole).

HIE TH protocol specifics (33-34 degC, 72 h, aEEG) - selective vs whole-body and time-window (within 6 h) are high-yield AU NICU practice. Distractor D (head cooling) is the genuine historic alternative.

Term neonatal seizure with normal ionised Ca (data check) - distractor B (empirical Ca) is the genuine trap rewarding data-table reading. Phenobarb first-line and pyridoxine-trial both valid sub-discriminations.
LISA vs INSURE vs await/CPAP-alone is current-evidence preterm RDS L4. Postnatal-betamethasone-to-infant distractor is appropriately absurd-by-design.
Five contacts each requiring different measles PEP (MMR vs NHIG vs none) per AIH - excellent multi-variant L4 calibrated to AU Australian Immunisation Handbook.

Immunocompromised varicella PEP - live vaccine contraindication + ZIG 96 h vs prophylactic valaciclovir alternative is L4. (Minor overlap with paeds-id-012 - consider differentiating exposure scenarios further if both kept.)

Near-duplicate of paeds-allergy-derm-019 (NF post-varicella). Distractors B (Kawasaki + IVIG/aspirin) and D (oral phenoxymethylpenicillin for scarlet fever) absurd given shock. Downgrade L5 -> L4 or rewrite distractors with credible NF management nuances (clinda inclusion, MRI before/after antibiotics, vanco choice in MSSA-suspected vs CA-MRSA areas).
Recurrent UTI investigation pathway: DMSA scar assessment vs MCUG-for-VUR is current AU paeds nephrology nuance - good L4.
PUV sequencing: catheter decompress first, then valve ablation (option A is genuine surgical-sequencing trap). Good L4.
Steroid-resistant NS at 4 weeks - biopsy + podocyte gene panel before second-line immunosuppression. Tacrolimus and rituximab distractors are credible second-line traps for the post-biopsy decision.
HUS anti-pattern teaching: avoid platelets, antibiotics, antimotility, plasma exchange. All four distractors test specific harm-avoidance knowledge - excellent L4.
Phenotypically male with bilateral non-palpable gonads = DSD workup not bilateral cryptorchidism. Distractor B is the genuine miss-it trap, distractor D the premature-treatment trap.
Intermittent torsion + bell-clapper anatomy = surgical exploration regardless of Doppler - high-yield anti-pattern (false-reassuring imaging) L5.
Severe asthma escalation to IV Mg; aminophylline alternative is genuine competitor. Some overlap with paeds-emerg-003 but separate exposure scenario.
Septic shock post-40 mL/kg crystalloid: adrenaline infusion vs intubation-first vs additional bolus is the genuine post-fluid-refractory escalation discussion.
Bronchiolitis with hypoxia + poor feeding: HFNP + NG vs salbutamol-trial (anti-pattern for under-12-mo bronchiolitis) is genuine L4.

Lead-in 'single best next step' but B bundles abx + resus. Don't-delay-antibiotics-for-LP-or-CT is L3-L4, not L5. Distractor E (IM benzylpen + observe in ED with IV access) is absurd. Downgrade L5 -> L4 or rewrite E to a more credible alternative.

Three distractors absurd (continue intermittent salb, discharge with pred when drowsy, cease bronchodilators). Single-concept escalation recognition. Optional rewrite: replace with credible IV-therapy alternatives (IV Mg vs IV salbutamol vs IV aminophylline vs early intubation).

7-week febrile infant = septic workup + IV abx. All four distractors implausible (oral cephalexin discharge, observe without abx, US first, paracetamol-reassess). Single-concept under-8-weeks rule. Already covered at L3 by paeds-acute-hy-001 (which is the proper question to keep).

Bacterial meningitis features = cultures + abx first. Three distractors absurd (observe, ibuprofen + discharge, CT before abx). Optional rewrite: differentiate dose / agent / abx-before-LP timing in credible ways.

Kawasaki pattern recognition with 6 days fever + classic features = IVIG + aspirin. Distractors C (measles + vit A), D (ceftriaxone for sepsis), E (delay until echo) are absurd given clinical picture. Optional rewrite: probe IVIG-resistance pathway (second-dose IVIG vs infliximab vs IV methylpred) or aspirin-dose nuance.

Symptomatic hyponatraemia (Na 119 + seizure) = 3% saline. All four distractors absurd. Optional rewrite: 3% saline dose (3 vs 5 mL/kg over 10 vs 30 min), rate-of-correction targets, when to add hydrocortisone, fluid restriction post-correction.
HSP severity-discriminator question; haematuria + hypertension (C) vs intussusception-risk-from-abdo-pain (E) is the genuine clinical discrimination. Good L4.
Testicular torsion with 5 h symptoms - US-first is the genuine plausible-but-wrong competitor. Manual-detorsion-and-discharge is the credible second trap.

Near-duplicate of both-finals-103 and paeds-batch-emerg-011 (meningococcal sepsis with classic shock features). Distractor E (notify PHU and await advice) absurd; A/C delay-for-LP/CT are textbook anti-patterns. Single-concept recall.

BRUE risk stratification + admission criteria + 12-lead ECG + targeted (not blanket) investigation - this is current RCH/AAP-aligned and option C (full septic workup) is a credible overkill trap. Good L4.
Chorio with inadequate IAP duration (2 h) + well infant - empirical abx + 36-48 h reassess vs newer observe-with-serial-bloods (option C) is genuine current-practice L4.
HSP nephritis with nephrotic-range proteinuria - biopsy + nephrology before empirical steroids. The 'steroids for HSP nephritis' empirical trap (B) is genuine high-yield AU nephrology nuance.
DIOS in CF - Gastrografin is established treatment. NG NAC (C) is the credible meconium-ileus-style trap. Good L4.

Extreme-leukocytosis pertussis = exchange transfusion to prevent pulmonary hypertension - genuine L5 critical-care pearl. Recommend ensuring the WBC count is shown explicitly in data_table (currently truncated in preview - verify the actual WBC value is shown so candidates can identify the leukocytosis trigger).
Oligoarticular JIA with ANA+ in a young girl - slit-lamp uveitis screening is high-yield. Distractors B/C/D/E are credible related-but-wrong tests.
Giant Kawasaki coronary aneurysm = warfarin + low-dose aspirin per AHA. Clopidogrel (A) is the credible second-line trap. Genuine L5.

verdicts.discard_reason: Near-identical to paeds-batch-031 (DIOS in CF, same Gastrografin answer, same distractors structure). Drop one of the two.
verdicts.fix_sketch: Either drop paeds-batch-056 or rewrite stem to a substantially different DIOS scenario (e.g. recurrent DIOS post-Gastrografin failure, or DIOS in young CF child requiring different agent).

verdicts.discard_reason: Near-identical to paeds-batch-033 (pertussis with extreme leukocytosis -> exchange transfusion). Drop one of the two.
verdicts.fix_sketch: Either drop paeds-batch-057 or rewrite stem to a substantially different pertussis scenario (e.g. household contact prophylaxis, or pertussis in pregnancy).

verdicts.discard_reason: Near-duplicate of paeds-emerg-003 and paeds-batch-emerg-002 (severe asthma -> IV magnesium, same distractor structure). Three near-identical L4 questions on the same pearl.
verdicts.fix_sketch: Keep one of the three asthma-IV-Mg questions; rewrite the others to discriminate next-step-after-Mg (IV salbutamol vs aminophylline vs intubation criteria).
Perianal Crohn's with transsphincteric fistula + abscess = infliximab. Seton + metronidazole (D) is genuine bridging step distractor. Good L4.

Urea cycle disorder pattern (hyperammonia + respiratory alkalosis from CNS hyperventilation, normal glucose, no acidosis) - sodium benzoate/phenylacetate + arginine is the high-yield drug-specific L5 answer.
Low C3 in nephrotic syndrome flags atypical glomerulonephritis (MPGN, lupus, post-infectious) requiring biopsy - genuine L4 atypical-features discriminator.
Sickle cell acute chest syndrome refractory to top-up = exchange transfusion. Simple-top-up (B) is the genuine plausible-but-wrong competitor.
Loculated empyema = intrapleural fibrinolytic before VATS escalation. BTS/AAP guideline-discrimination, genuine L4.
Refractory anaphylaxis on maxed adrenaline; methylene-blue (A) vs vasopressin (D) is a genuine PICU vasoplegia rescue debate - L5 appropriate.

Button battery in oesophagus = honey + endoscopy. Distractors C (ipecac), D (glucagon), E (carbonated beverage) absurd. B (observe + repeat X-ray) is the only weakly credible trap. Single-concept recall.

MIS-C vs Kawasaki Shock Syndrome vs septic shock is a genuine clinical differential triplet - good L4. Recommend stem add explicit COVID exposure / SARS-CoV-2 serology context to strengthen MIS-C diagnosis.
Anti-NMDAR encephalitis + ovarian teratoma + first-line immunotherapy + tumour removal. Aciclovir-empirical bridge (C) is the credible alternative pre-PCR. Excellent L5.
Wilson disease pattern (low ALP, ALP:bilirubin ratio, Coombs-negative haemolysis) + ceruloplasmin/24-h Cu. AIH (B) is the genuine differential trap. Good L4.

Refeeding pattern day 3 = hold calories + IV electrolyte replacement. All four distractors absurd (increase calories, frusemide for refeeding oedema, cease all intake, albumin transfusion). Optional rewrite: replace distractors with credible alternatives (continue calories with parallel IV replacement; NG vs oral phosphate; aggressive 1 mmol/kg/h vs cautious 0.3 mmol/kg/h phosphate).

Lithium-in-pregnancy planning with prior multi-mood-stabiliser failures - foetal echo at 18-20 wk, lowest effective dose, peripartum dose reduction, lactation planning. Option C bundles too many wrong claims (could be split). A, B, D are credible plausible-but-wrong alternatives. Genuine L5.

High-acuity SI with access to firearm + plan + intent = remove means + involuntary admission. All four distractors absurd (helpline + discharge, dose-up SSRI clinic 1w, IM olanzapine no indication, no-suicide contract). Single-concept recall.
Lithium toxicity triple-drug interaction (indapamide + naproxen + ACEi) + dialysis indication. Charcoal-doesn't-bind-lithium (A) and partial-drug-cessation (D) are credible high-yield traps.

Postpartum psychosis recognition + MBU admission. All four distractors absurd (community sertraline, HSV encephalitis empirical, diazepam discharge, EPDS in 2 weeks). Optional rewrite: probe MBU vs general psych admission, choice of antipsychotic with lactation safety, role of lithium adjunct.

Atypical depression features (mood reactivity, hypersomnia, hyperphagia, leaden paralysis, rejection sensitivity) -> MAOI is high-yield niche knowledge. Mirtazapine swap (D) is credible alternative competitor. Good L4.
BPD low-lethality presentation - safety plan + community DBT, NOT brief admission (iatrogenic harm). Option B (48 h admission) is the credible reflex-admission trap. Good L4.

Hyperactive-hypoactive overlap; on day-3 ceftriaxone + hyponatraemia + new opioid = delirium not depression. SSRI (A), haloperidol (B), CT (D) and ECT (E) are all clearly distractor caricatures. Optional rewrite: differentiate which intervention sequence (treat infection + correct Na vs withhold opioid first vs both simultaneously).
Capacity is decision-specific, not test-score-based or diagnosis-based. Options A (MoCA<26) and B (dementia diagnosis) are real-world clinical misconceptions worth testing. Good L4.
Post-delirium discharge-destination capacity reassessment in dementia. EPOG-overrides-refusal (A) is the credible real-world misconception trap. Good L5.
Olanzapine-induced metabolic syndrome + cross-taper to lower-metabolic antipsychotic. B (halve olanzapine + monitor without other change) is the credible minimum-change trap.

Serotonin syndrome from over-65 citalopram dose cap (TGA 20 mg max) + tramadol. Option B (NMS) is a credible differential and option A is correct with the citalopram-cap-specific reasoning. Option lengths are uneven (A and B much longer than C/D/E) - consider trimming A or padding C/D/E to reduce length cueing.

Serotonin syndrome (sertraline + tramadol) with clonus, hyperreflexia, no rigidity. Bromocriptine (NMS), benztropine (EPS), dantrolene (MH/NMS) distractors test toxidrome-specific Rx knowledge. Good L4.
Clozapine myocarditis in week 2-3 of titration + raised troponin/S3 = cease + cardiology + echo. Neutrophil-trap-as-agranulocytosis (C) is credible clozapine-pearl distractor. Good L4.
Alcohol withdrawal with seizure/DT history + symptom-triggered diazepam + Pabrinex. Under-treatment trap (A: thiamine + observe alone) is credible. Good L4.
OST induction with bup-nal at moderate COWS; methadone-first-day-60-mg (B) is the high-yield mortality-risk trap. Naltrexone-after-24-h (C) tests minimum opioid-free interval knowledge. Good L4.

Methadone QTc prolongation + CYP-interacting fluconazole + sedating antihistamine. Stop-the-precipitants vs methadone-dose adjustment vs beta-blocker myth (E) are credible AU OST-prescribing L4 traps.

Meth-induced acute behavioural disturbance: droperidol + cautious benzo combination per RCH/RACP sedation pathway. Haloperidol-mono-no-benzo (D) and ketamine-first-line (C) are credible escalation-sequence traps.

Bipolar mania off lithium 3 weeks = MHA detain. Distractors B (discharge), C (common-law restraint), E (AMA discharge) absurd. D (CTO without inpatient first) is the only weakly credible alternative. Optional rewrite: probe which MHA schedule timing/duration, role of CTO vs inpatient admission for first severe relapse, ED holding-pending-bed protocol.

DT escalation = ICU + IV benzo + electrolytes. All four distractors clearly wrong (continue oral + reassess 4 h, haloperidol primary, phenytoin for AWS seizures, IM naloxone). Optional rewrite: probe IV diazepam dose-titration target, propofol second-line escalation criteria, MgSO4 role, dexmedetomidine adjunct.
